title: What a godly man does with anger
caption:
A godly man still gets angry.

The question was never whether he'd feel it. The question is what he does with it.

Most men do one of two things: they bury it (quiet on the outside, rotting on the inside) or they explode it (righteous-sounding words, wrong-aimed fire). Both are misses.

Anger is a signal. Before you react, ask what it's pointing at. There's something underneath the heat — pain, usually, or fear, or something you care about more than you've admitted. Name that first.

Then decide. Because "be angry and do not sin" (Ephesians 4:26) doesn't say stop being angry. It says govern what you do with it.

Anger in a governed man is useful. In an ungoverned one, it's a weapon he turns on everyone who loves him.

#faith #christianmen #godlymen #manofgod #disciplineequalsfreedom #selfcontrol #anger #christianity #scripture
theme: He runs his life — his impulses don't control him.
